headline: OCC Faces Senate Scrutiny Over 3 Crypto Charters

article.title: Senator Warren questions OCC chief on approval of ‘ineligible’ crypto trust charters
article.summary: The Massachusetts lawmaker asked the US Comptroller of the Currency to provide all communications between the agency and Donald Trump potentially related to the charter approvals.

headline: BoE: Tokenization to Cut 30% Trading Costs

article.title: BoE deputy says tokenization could cut costs, boost competition
article.summary: The UK central bank said digital money should remain trusted and interoperable as it weighs stablecoin reforms and near-24/7 settlement to support tokenized markets.

headline: Junk Bonds Sink 3% on Rate Fears

article.title: Investor Angst Emerges in Riskiest Stocks on Rate Uncertainty
article.summary: Investors are starting to hedge the risk of a drop in the Russell 2000 Index of smaller companies, despite investors remaining bullish on large-cap stocks.

article.title: Stocks Fall as Fed-Hike Anxiety Lifts Bond Yields: Markets Wrap
article.summary: The latest bout of weakness in bonds lifted yields to multi-year highs, reducing the appetite for stocks on speculation that a torrid market surge has gone too far amid war-fueled inflation risks.

headline: Warren Challenges 3 Crypto Bank Charter Approvals

article.title: Elizabeth Warren Calls Crypto Bank Charter Approvals for Firms Like Coinbase, Ripple Illegal
article.summary: In a letter to the Comptroller of the Currency, Warren argued nine national trust bank charter approvals for crypto firms violated the National Bank Act.

headline: 90% Lie to AI, Crypto Fraud Spikes

article.title: People Are More Willing to Lie to AI Than Humans, Study Finds
article.summary: Consumers feel less social pressure when lying to an AI chatbot, research finds, while human-like social cues reduce dishonest behavior.

headline: CFTC Sues Minnesota Over $1B Crypto Market Ban

article.title: CFTC sues Minnesota over first explicit state ban on prediction markets
article.summary: Minnesota’s law could expose exchanges, payment providers, media partners, and sports leagues to criminal liability.

headline: Stablecoin supply hits $300 billion, growth slows 30%.

article.summary: The bank-issued and GENIUS Act-compliant stablecoin entrants have had a harder start than many expected.

headline: Oil Surges 5% on Iran Strike Fears

article.summary: US president warns Tehran has ‘two or three days’ to reach a deal and reopen the Strait of Hormuz
